raw_text:
Yes — start Wave 2 now, in parallel with finishing the 3 in-progress tail beads, but do it in the right order to avoid rework.

Wave 2: Start Now (with dependency-safe ordering)
1) Immediate starts (no dependency on the in-progress beads)

These are pure unlock work and should begin immediately:

bd-17s3 — scaffold tools/projection-rebuild

bd-17s4 — scaffold tools/tenantctl

bd-17a2 — deterministic projection rebuild tool (will need bd-17s3, but not a3/b2/b4)

bd-17c1 — tenant provisioning CLI (will need bd-17s4)

bd-17c2 — fleet migration runner (will need bd-17s4)

Why: these are the next critical unlocks, and they don’t depend on a3/b2/b4.

2) Start once their prerequisites land

bd-17a4 (replay certification) → start after bd-17a2 is merged

bd-17c3 (tenant suspend/deprovision) → start after bd-17c1 and bd-17b0 (already done)

bd-17c4 (100-tenant scale simulation) → start after bd-17a4 + bd-17b4 + bd-17c2 (so it’s a Wave 3 capstone)

3) Do NOT start early

bd-18b0/b1/d0/e0/c1: keep blocked until Phase 17 dependencies are truly merged and stable.

Assignment suggestion (max throughput, minimal collisions)
Parallel lane A (Projection)

Agent: MaroonHarbor or CopperRiver

Start bd-17s3

Then bd-17a2

Then bd-17a4

Parallel lane B (Tenant)

Agent: PurpleCliff

Start bd-17s4

Then bd-17c1

Then bd-17c2

Parallel lane C (Finish Wave 1 tails)

Keep current owners finishing:

bd-17a3 (fallback) — keep MaroonHarbor if already deep

bd-17b2 (diff audit) — PurpleCliff

bd-17b4 (audit completeness oracle) — CopperRiver

If you only have 3 agents total, prioritize: bd-17s3 + bd-17s4 + bd-17a2 first, and let a3/b2/b4 finish as they finish.

Stop-the-line checks before merging bd-17s3 / bd-17s4

cargo test --workspace green

tools crates do not depend on module crates directly (they can call HTTP endpoints / use platform crates, but avoid linking module internals)

no new heavy deps added casually (tokio/reqwest ok; keep it lean)

Proceed with Wave 2 unlocks now.
